title: fix: sync device flow scopes into key permissions
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Scope it out
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky bug where device flow scopes weren't actually attaching to your shiny new API keys. Now your keys have exactly the permissions they asked for!
gist.summary: Fixed an issue in the device authorization flow where requested scopes were displayed on the consent screen but not properly applied to the generated API key.
gist.impact: API keys created via the device authorization flow will now correctly inherit their requested account permissions, ensuring proper access control.
gist.visual_concept: The PR is about API keys, permissions, and scopes. Visual metaphor: The bee (Polly) crafting or inspecting a shiny golden key, making sure it has the right 'teeth' (scopes/permissions). A glowing lock or a security badge fits well.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny security guard hat, carefully inspecting a glowing golden key on a wooden workbench. A chunky retro monitor in the background displays green checkmarks next to a list of permissions. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
